\text{Let } \alpha = \cos^{-1}\left(\frac{7}{74} \, \sqrt{74}\right) \text{ and } \beta = \sin^{-1}\left(-\frac{5}{41} \, \sqrt{41}\right).

\text{Based on principal domains, } \alpha \text{ is in Quadrant } I \text{ and } \beta \text{ is in Quadrant } IV.

\text{Using the reference triangles, we identify the sides:}

\text{For } \alpha: x = 7, \quad y = 5, \quad r = \sqrt{74}

\text{For } \beta: x = 4, \quad y = -5, \quad r = \sqrt{41}

\text{Evaluate the fundamental trigonometric ratios:}

\sin(\alpha) = \frac{5}{74} \, \sqrt{74}, \quad \cos(\alpha) = \frac{7}{74} \, \sqrt{74}

\sin(\beta) = -\frac{5}{41} \, \sqrt{41}, \quad \cos(\beta) = \frac{4}{41} \, \sqrt{41}

\text{Apply the sum/difference identity:}

\sin(\alpha - \beta) = \sin(\alpha)\cos(\beta) - \cos(\alpha)\sin(\beta)

\sin(\alpha - \beta) = \left(\frac{5}{74} \, \sqrt{74}\right)\left(\frac{4}{41} \, \sqrt{41}\right) - \left(\frac{7}{74} \, \sqrt{74}\right)\left(-\frac{5}{41} \, \sqrt{41}\right)

\sin(\alpha - \beta) = \frac{55}{3034} \, \sqrt{74} \sqrt{41}
